keyPoints: IPC stands for Inter-process communication, used to enable communication between two or more processes.
Pipes are a type of IPC that uses a pipe as a connection between two processes.
Popen and pclose are used to create a new process and close the process using a pipe.
Coprocesses are a type of IPC that allows a process to use the CPU of another process.
FIFO stands for First-In-First-Out, a type of IPC that uses a file to store data.
System V IPC refers to the Inter-Process Communication methods developed by Unix System V.
Message Queues are a type of IPC that allows one process to send a message to another process.
Semaphores are a type of IPC that allows one process to control access to a resource.
